question.text: In the SEBI case against M/s Tradebulls Securities Private Limited, what was the monetary penalty imposed for violations related to KYC compliance?

answer.text: Rs. 10,00,000 (Ten Lakhs) under Section 15HB of the SEBI Act
Rs. 5,00,000 (Five Lakhs) under Section 15HA of the SEBI Act
Rs. 1,00,000 (One Lakh) under Section 15HB of the SEBI Act
Rs. 25,00,000 (Twenty-Five Lakhs) under Section 15HB of the SEBI Act

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In the case of M/s Tradebulls Securities Private Limited, the Adjudicating Officer imposed a penalty of Rs. 10,00,000 (Rupees Ten Lakh only) under Section 15HB of the SEBI Act, 1992, for violations including delays in uploading KYC documents to KRA, non-compliance with KYC form requirements, and deficiencies in In-Person Verification (IPV) procedures. **The Trap:** Candidates may confuse this with the Rs. 1 lakh penalty imposed on Aadinath Securities in a similar case. The penalty amounts differ based on the severity and nature of violations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 15HB is the catch-all penalty provision for contraventions where no separate penalty has been provided, with penalties extendable up to Rs. 1 crore.
